tavily.raw: {"query":"Wegovy international market launch timeline 2026 regulatory approvals country list","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.regulatoryrapporteur.org/industry-news/ema-recommends-marketing-authorisation-extension-for-wegovy-tablets/1115.article","title":"EMA recommends marketing authorisation extension for Wegovy tablets | Regulatory Rapporteur","content":"* Regulatory Rapporteur - July/August 2026. A career in veterinary regulatory affairs: Roles and competencies across the product lifecycle. The European Medicines Agency (EMA) has endorsed an extension to the marketing authorisation of Wegovy (semaglutide) in the EU for weight management. The extension enables the use of tablets, making it the first glucagon-like peptide (GLP-1) receptor agonist for weight management developed for oral use. The tablets offer a more convenient approach for patients compared to injections and, as with the injections, are intended to be used for people who are overweight or obese, or for those who have at least one weight-related comorbidity like cardiovascular disease, type 2 diabetes or obstructive sleep apnoea. The recommendation adopted by the Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) will be sent to the European Commission for a decision on the extension. Novo Nordisk, the marketing authorisation holder for Wegovy, has indicated that it plans to launch the tablets in select EU markets in the second half of 2026.","score":0.7938904,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.pearceip.law/2025/07/24/dr-reddys-plans-to-launch-generic-semaglutide-in-87-countries-in-2026","title":"Dr Reddy’s Plans to Launch Generic Semaglutide in 87 Countries in 2026 | Pearce IP","content":"# Dr Reddy’s Plans to Launch Generic Semaglutide in 87 Countries in 2026. On 24 July 2025, *Reuters* reported that Dr Reddy’s CEO, Erez Israeli, stated the company plans to launch its generic version of Novo Nordisk’s Wegovy® (semaglutide), in 87 countries in 2026, beginning with Canada, India, Brazil, Turkey and other emerging markets, subject to patent expiry. Israeli stated that the “U.S. and Europe will open later… (and) all the other Western markets will be open between 2029 to 2033”, and that he expects Dr Reddy’s generic semaglutide to generate “hundreds of millions of dollars” in sales. According to *Reuters*, other Indian drugmakers, including Cipla, Lupin, Biocon and Sun Pharma also plan to launch generic weight-loss drugs following the success of Novo Nordisk’s Wegovy® and Eli Lilly’s Mounjaro® (tirzepatide).
